title: potentilla polymorpha var. cinerea
intro: Potentilla polymorpha var. cinerea, also known as Silver Buffalo Berry, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its silver-gray leaves and edible red berries. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of salicylic acid and flavonoids. The health benefits of Potentilla polymorpha var. cinerea include its ability to treat fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its berries can be eaten raw or used in jams and preserves, making it a versatile and valuable herbal remedy.
